title: calystegia silvatica subsp. silvatica
intro: Calystegia silvatica subsp. silvatica, also known as wild morning glory, is a perennial flowering plant native to North America. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including anxiety, insomnia, and inflammation, due to its medicinal properties such as antispasmodic and sedative effects. The plant's roots and leaves have been used in herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, to alleviate symptoms of stress and promote relaxation, as well as to support digestive health. Additionally, the plant's antioxidant properties may help to protect against oxidative stress and promote overall well-being.
